title: ulmus campestris subsp. procera
intro: Ulmus campestris subsp. procera, also known as the tall elm or wych elm, is a subspecies of elm tree native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been reported to help treat various health conditions such as colds, fever, and sore throats. The health benefits of Ulmus campestris subsp. procera include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and provide antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation of the plant is typically made by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
